Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for threads related to "Kendra Lawson" or "comp hold" or "ownership" or "commission hold" (or similar) using search_emails to discover Hana Kim's own email to Grace Yamamoto placing the Kendra Lawson commission payout on hold pending Grace's ownership decision between Carlos Rivera and Derek Moss, Carlos's email to Grace documenting his timeline and Alicia Grant referral, and Derek's email to Grace asserting he received a direct call from Kendra before any realtor introduction.
OE 2: Search emails for threads related to "Mercer" or "lock extension" or "rate lock" or "builder delay" (or similar) using search_emails to discover Evan Mercer's email to Carlos Rivera disputing the second lock extension fee and refusing to pay another $1,500 because the delay is builder-side, Lauren Mitchell's email about the six-week permit delay pushing estimated completion to June 12, 2026, and Carlos's email to Lauren requesting a firmer completion date.
OE 3: Search emails for threads related to "Marcus Webb" or "departed" or "departure" or "departing LO" (or similar) using search_emails to discover Denise Holloway's email to outside counsel Laura Bennett about Marcus Webb submitting notice, leaving for another brokerage, and the after-hours email forwarding of borrower data to a personal address.
OE 4: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Kendra Lawson" or "comp hold" or "ownership" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Hana's message putting Kendra Lawson comp on hold until Grace rules on ownership, Carlos's messages about setting up LN-2026-00192 from the Alicia Grant referral and asking why Kendra is showing under Derek in pipeline, and Grace's message pulling Kendra into review.
OE 5: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Mercer" or "lock extension" or "lock ext" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find messages about the first lock extension fee logged on LN-2026-00184, Evan's pushback on the second extension, Hana setting up a pending $375 hold in QuickBooks, and Lauren's notification of the Fairview permit delay pushing completion past the current lock.
OE 6: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "Webb" or "departure" or "departed" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find messages about the Danielle Webb conflict of interest flag on multiple Marcus files, the Webb departure review calendar hold, and Robert's briefing about the Webb situation.
OE 7: Look up the Kendra Lawson loan in the mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00192") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00192") after discovering the loan number from email or Slack artifacts in OE 1 or OE 4. Note: searching by "Kendra Lawson" in the LOS will not return results because the borrower on record is Daniel Flores, not Kendra Lawson. The agent should find that loan LN-2026-00192 has borrower Daniel Flores, assigned LO Veronica Hayes who is terminated (termination date 2025-09-30, is_active: false), loan status denied, loan type FHA, and loan amount $181,000.
OE 8: Search the mortgage LOS for the Mercer loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" or similar) to discover loan LN-2026-00184. The agent should find borrower Evan Mercer, loan amount $340,000, purchase price $425,000, conventional loan, assigned LO Carlos Rivera, status processing, rate lock from 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-23 (expired), property at 6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte, NC 28210.
OE 9: Search the CRM for Kendra Lawson using crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kendra Lawson") to find lead crm_lead_721fb6e49da5 with status "new", lead source "Realtor referral - Alicia Grant", created 2026-03-24, and notes mentioning she was sent over by Alicia Grant. Also search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to find multiple engagement notes documenting Carlos's referral claim, Derek's direct-call claim, Jordan mediating, and the comp hold pending Grace's assignment decision.
OE 10: Search Stripe charges for the Mercer lock extension payments using stripe_search_charges (query: "Mercer" or "lock extension" or similar) or stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_scenario_c66_mercer") to discover three succeeded charges against customer Evan Mercer (cus_scenario_c66_mercer): ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 for $375.00 (1st lock extension), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 for $375.00 (2nd lock extension), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 for $500.00 (3rd lock extension), totaling $1,250.00 collected. The agent should also check stripe_list_refunds to confirm no refunds exist on these charges.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for lock extension bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to "LOCK-EXT" or "Mercer" or similar, or fetchAll: true) to discover three bills: BILL-2026-0319-001 (DocNumber LOCK-EXT-00184) for $375 with balance $375 and empty line items, BILL-2026-0402-001 (DocNumber LOCKEXT-00184-1) for $375 with balance $0 (paid) and line item describing "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay", and BILL-2026-0416-002 (DocNumber PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER) for $375 with balance $375 and line item describing "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan." Total billed is $1,125.00, creating a $125.00 discrepancy against $1,250.00 collected in Stripe (the 3rd Stripe charge was $500 but no corresponding $500 bill exists in QuickBooks).
OE 12: Search Stripe connected accounts using stripe_list_connected_accounts to identify the departed LO's payout account. The agent should discover acct_scenario_644_departed belonging to "Marcus Webb Consulting" with email marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com and metadata showing staff_id "los_staff_a583f044387a" and departed date "2026-03-15", with charges and payouts still enabled.
OE 13: List Stripe transfers to the departed LO's account using stripe_list_transfers (destination_account_id: "acct_scenario_644_departed") to discover five commission transfers totaling $7,655.00: tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn for $1,250.00 (Webb -- Chen, LN-2026-00525), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 for $1,875.00 (Webb -- Patel, LN-2026-00527), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 for $980.00 (Webb -- Torres, LN-2026-00008), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs for $1,450.00 (Webb -- Kim, LN-2026-00211) with metadata auto_generated: true, and tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA for $2,100.00 (Webb -- Okafor, LN-2026-00608) with metadata auto_generated: true. Two of the five transfers posted automatically after the departure date as indicated by the auto_generated flag.
OE 14: Verify Marcus Webb's LOS staff status using mortgage_los_list_staff (role: "loan_officer") or mortgage_los_get_staff (staff_id related to Marcus Webb) to discover that los_staff_a583f044387a (Marcus Webb) still shows is_active: true and termination_date: null in the LOS, contradicting the Stripe connected account metadata that shows departed 2026-03-15.
OE 15: Search the mortgage LOS for the loan files referenced in the Webb transfers using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan for LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00608 to discover that none of these loans are currently assigned to Marcus Webb in the LOS. LN-2026-00525 (assigned to Derek Moss, status: processing), LN-2026-00527 (assigned to Derek Moss, status: underwriting), LN-2026-00008 (assigned to Derek Moss, status: conditional_approval), LN-2026-00211 (assigned to Brian Mitchell who is also terminated, status: withdrawn), and LN-2026-00608 (assigned to James Thornton, status: closed).
OE 16: Look up Grace Yamamoto's and Hana Kim's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") to confirm Grace's email is grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and Hana's email is hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com for use in the outgoing email.
OE 17: Search Stripe transfers using stripe_list_transfers to discover transfer tr_scenario_e8f_disputed for $2,850.00 described as "LO commission -- Chen/Moss referral split -- Patel (LN-2026-00192)" with metadata showing disputed: true, claimant_1: "Amy Chen", and claimant_2: "Derek Moss". This is the payout amount in records that needs to be verified against the file.
OE 18: Send an email using send_email with sender hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com and recipient grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com summarizing the status of all three month-end close items and flagging items needing Grace's sign-off: (1) Kendra Lawson comp remains on hold pending Grace's ownership ruling between Carlos and Derek, with the disputed Stripe transfer of $2,850 on LN-2026-00192 and the LOS showing the loan as denied with a different borrower (Daniel Flores) and a terminated LO (Veronica Hayes); (2) Mercer lock extension reconciliation showing $1,250 collected in Stripe across three charges versus $1,125 billed in QuickBooks across three bills with a $125 gap, plus borrower Evan Mercer disputing the extension fees; (3) five commission transfers totaling $7,655 to Marcus Webb's departed account (acct_scenario_644_departed, departure date 2026-03-15), of which two totaling $3,550 were auto-generated after departure and the LOS still shows Webb as active.
OE 19: Add an activity note to the Kendra Lawson loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for LN-2026-00192, action: "note") with detail summarizing the commission hold status, ownership dispute between Carlos and Derek, the disputed Stripe transfer of $2,850, and that the matter is pending Grace's assignment decision.
OE 20: Add an activity note to the Mercer loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id for LN-2026-00184, action: "note") with detail summarizing the lock extension reconciliation findings: three Stripe charges totaling $1,250 versus three QuickBooks bills totaling $1,125, the $125 discrepancy, and the borrower's dispute of the extension fees.
OE 21: Add activity notes to the Webb-associated loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity (action: "note") for the relevant loan files among LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, and LN-2026-00608 with detail noting the commission transfers sent to Marcus Webb's departed account and flagging the two auto-generated transfers on LN-2026-00211 ($1,450) and LN-2026-00608 ($2,100).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
